channel.title: yourfdc.au News — Family Day Care Australia
channel.description: Australia's dedicated Family Day Care news hub.

item.title: OWNA Group acquires Enrolment Hub to deliver end-to-end outcomes for Australia's early education sector
item.description: Combination of leading childcare management software and specialist enrolment services creates a single trusted partner for childcare centres across Australia.
item.content:encoded: Combination of leading childcare management software and specialist enrolment services creates a single trusted partner for childcare centres across Australia.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Inclusion Support Program: Inclusion Agencies confirmed from July 2026
item.description: The Australian Government has confirmed the organisations that will deliver Inclusion Agency (IA) services under the Inclusion Support Program (ISP) from 1 July 2026, providing certainty for early childhood education and care (ECEC) services ahead of the new contract period.
item.content:encoded: The Australian Government has confirmed the organisations that will deliver Inclusion Agency (IA) services under the Inclusion Support Program (ISP) from 1 July 2026, providing certainty for early childhood education and care (ECEC) services ahead of the new contract period.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: CCS hourly rate caps and income thresholds set to rise from 6 July 2026
item.description: Early childhood education and care (ECEC) providers and families will see increases to Child Care Subsidy (CCS) hourly rate caps and family income thresholds from 6 July 2026, following the Australian Government's annual indexation process.
item.content:encoded: Early childhood education and care (ECEC) providers and families will see increases to Child Care Subsidy (CCS) hourly rate caps and family income thresholds from 6 July 2026, following the Australian Government's annual indexation process.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: How active play supports child safety
item.description: With national child safety reforms landing, services are having to re-examine daily practice. Here’s why educator-led energetic play is one of your strongest safeguards under QA2.
item.content:encoded: With national child safety reforms landing, services are having to re-examine daily practice. Here’s why educator-led energetic play is one of your strongest safeguards under QA2.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Blairmount Kids Learning Academy takes a lead on childcare safety with AI-powered supervision
item.description: Blairmount Kids Learning Academy is participating in the Australian Government's National CCTV Assessment Program following its integration of an AI-based solution from Safe-Sight.ai, aimed at strengthening supervision and safety within its centres.
item.content:encoded: Blairmount Kids Learning Academy is participating in the Australian Government's National CCTV Assessment Program following its integration of an AI-based solution from Safe-Sight.ai, aimed at strengthening supervision and safety within its centres.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Early childhood educators burnt out, stretched and 'paid peanuts', experts say - Australian Broadcasting Corporation
item.description: Early childhood educators burnt out, stretched and 'paid peanuts', experts say - Australian Broadcasting Corporation
item.content:encoded: Early childhood educators burnt out, stretched and 'paid peanuts', experts say - Australian Broadcasting Corporation
item.dc:creator: Australian Broadcasting Corporation

item.title: Red Nose opens Sydney hub to strengthen bereavement support for families
item.description: Red Nose Australia has opened a new Sydney Hub in Gladesville, creating a dedicated space for families grieving the loss of a baby or child to access face-to-face counselling, peer support and community-based bereavement programs.
item.content:encoded: Red Nose Australia has opened a new Sydney Hub in Gladesville, creating a dedicated space for families grieving the loss of a baby or child to access face-to-face counselling, peer support and community-based bereavement programs.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 12 June 2026
item.description: SNAICC responds to NT and Queensland child protection reforms, national coverage of the Child Placement Principle, the Youth Voice Engagement Report and AbSec’s NSW call.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC responds to NT and Queensland child protection reforms, national coverage of the Child Placement Principle, the Youth Voice Engagement Report and AbSec’s NSW call.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Report shows how young people want to use their voice for change
item.description: Young Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people want authentic representation, decision-making power and community-led structures that deliver real outcomes, according to a landmark national report released today.
item.content:encoded: Young Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people want authentic representation, decision-making power and community-led structures that deliver real outcomes, according to a landmark national report released today.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples ready to work in partnership for a safer future for Queensland children
item.description: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children CEO Catherine Liddle has urged the Queensland Government to work in genuine partnership with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people and communities in reforming the State’s child protection system.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children CEO Catherine Liddle has urged the Queensland Government to work in genuine partnership with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people and communities in reforming the State’s child protection system.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 5 June 2026
item.description: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, SNAICC responds to proposed NT child protection laws and the Children’s Commissioner’s report, and announces Marlee Silva as 2026 Children’s Day Ambassador.
item.content:encoded: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, SNAICC responds to proposed NT child protection laws and the Children’s Commissioner’s report, and announces Marlee Silva as 2026 Children’s Day Ambassador.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: SNAICC warns proposed NT child protection laws will make children less safe
item.description: SNAICC has warned that the Northern Territory Government's proposed NT child protection laws will make children less safe.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC has warned that the Northern Territory Government's proposed NT child protection laws will make children less safe.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Writer, Podcaster and TV Presenter, Marlee Silva to be 2026 Children’s Day Ambassador
item.description: SNAICC welcomes Marlee Silva as the 2026 National Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Children’s Day Ambassador.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC welcomes Marlee Silva as the 2026 National Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Children’s Day Ambassador.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 29 May 2026
item.description: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, SNAICC responds to NT child protection reforms, National Sorry Day, AbSec’s call for systemic change, the launch of the Bushland Animal Yoga Cards, and continued Budget coverage.
item.content:encoded: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, SNAICC responds to NT child protection reforms, National Sorry Day, AbSec’s call for systemic change, the launch of the Bushland Animal Yoga Cards, and continued Budget coverage.

item.description: ACECQA Incident Response Template strengthens child safe governance expectations across ECEC - The Sector
item.content:encoded: ACECQA Incident Response Template strengthens child safe governance expectations across ECEC - The Sector
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: From Country to Classroom: SNAICC Launches Culturally Grounded Learning Tools
item.description: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children has launched a unique culturally informed early Childhood Education and Care resource for early childhood workers. The Bushland Animal Yoga Cards’ offer ACCO and non-Indigenous ECEC services a resource that incorporates movement with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander languages, with native Australian animals representing various poses and movements.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children has launched a unique culturally informed early Childhood Education and Care resource for early childhood workers. The Bushland Animal Yoga Cards’ offer ACCO and non-Indigenous ECEC services a resource that incorporates movement with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander languages, with native Australian animals representing various poses and movements.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: To improve children’s mental health, start by supporting their parents
item.description: Long-term data shows children are more likely to develop mental health issues when their parents experience financial stress and housing instability.
item.content:encoded: Long-term data shows children are more likely to develop mental health issues when their parents experience financial stress and housing instability.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Leadership Calls for Real Action on Child Safety
item.description: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander leaders call for real action on child safety; warn NT law changes undermine Child Placement Principle.
item.content:encoded: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander leaders call for real action on child safety; warn NT law changes undermine Child Placement Principle.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: SNAICC Raises Alarm Over Narrow NT Child Protection Inquiry Scope
item.description: SNAICC says narrow scope of NT Child Protection Inquiry fail to address systemic issues needed to keep Aboriginal children and families safe.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC says narrow scope of NT Child Protection Inquiry fail to address systemic issues needed to keep Aboriginal children and families safe.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Is baby talk bad? Why ‘parentese’ actually helps babies learn language
item.description: Exaggerating phrases and talking in a sing-song way can actually help, not make it harder, for young children to master speaking a language.
item.content:encoded: Exaggerating phrases and talking in a sing-song way can actually help, not make it harder, for young children to master speaking a language.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation
